description: High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2
reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2, a concrete implementation for file I/O. This couples the high-level business logic of the queue to a specific storage implementation, violating the Dependency Inversion Principle.

description: High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2
reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2. It should depend on an abstraction (e.g., PageIO interface) and receive an implementation factory or provider.

The DeadLetterQueueWriter directly instantiates the concrete class RecordIOWriter. This creates a hard-coded dependency on a low-level detail, preventing the use of alternative writers and complicating unit testing.

description: High-level factory directly instantiates concrete plugin delegator classes
reasoning: The factory explicitly instantiates OutputDelegatorExt, filterDelegatorClass, and other concrete plugin implementations. It should depend on an abstraction or a registered provider rather than concrete classes.
